query: Port of Baltimore U.S. Coast Guard security assessment consecutive top rating 2025 state-owned marine terminals

mako.raw:
{"query":"Port of Baltimore U.S. Coast Guard security assessment consecutive top rating 2025 state-owned marine terminals","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1772,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://content.govdelivery.com/accounts/MDMPA/bulletins/418644e","title":"Port of Baltimore Receives Top Coast Guard Security Assessment for 17th Consecutive Year","content":"MDOT Maryland Port Administration sent this bulletin at 05/26/2026 01:02 PM EDT
The Maryland Port Administration (MPA) - managed public terminals of the Helen Delich Bentley Port of Baltimore received a top annual security assessment from the U.S.
Coast Guard for the 17th consecutive year.
Protecting the state-owned, public terminals is vital to continuing the Port of Baltimore’s ongoing recovery from the Francis Scott Key Bridge incident and maintaining safe and effective operations.
Coast Guard Sector Maryland conducts an annual inspection at the Port of Baltimore’s public terminals to ensure compliance with federal security regulations.
The Port of Baltimore handled approximately 50 million tons of cargo in 2025, it’s second-best year ever.
Overall, Baltimore ranked 10th among U.S. ports for foreign cargo value and 11th for total tonnage."},{"url":"https://foxbaltimore.com/news/local/port-of-baltimore-top-security-rating","title":"Port of Baltimore earns top security rating for 16th consecutive year","content":"Port of Baltimore earns top security rating for 16th consecutive year
Coast Guard for its six state-owned public marine terminals, officials announced Thursday.
The port ranks tenth nationally for the dollar value of its cargo and eleventh for cargo tonnage, generating more than 20,300 direct jobs and connecting nearly 273,000 jobs in Maryland."},{"url":"https://www.ism-isps.com/details.php?term=USBAL-0001","title":"LOCODE & Port Facility Locator","content":"Details about Baltimore Port Area (USBAL-0001)
Baltimore Port Area (The boundary of the Baltimore COTP Zone, and the Baltimore Marine Inspection Zone, starts at a point 75°30’ W. longitude on the Delaware-Maryland boundary and proceeds along the Delaware-Maryland boundary west and north to the Pennsylvania boundary; then west along the Pennsylvania-Maryland boundary to the West Virginia boundary; then southerly and easterly along the Maryland-West Virginia boundary to the Virginia boundary; then southeasterly along the Maryland-Virginia boundary and the District of Columbia-Virginia boundary, as those boundaries are formed along the southern bank of the Potomac River; then easterly along the Maryland-Virginia boundary, as it proceeds across the Chesapeake Bay, Tangier and Pocomoke Sounds, Pocomoke River, and Delmarva Peninsula to a point 75°30’ W. longitude on the Maryland-Virginia boundary; then northerly to a point 75°30’ W. longitude on the Delaware-Maryland boundary.)"}]}
